title: Shadow Testing Agent Updates
concept: Running a new agent version in parallel with the production version to compare outputs on live traffic.

exercises.prompt: What is a circuit breaker in AI agent tools?

options.text: A tool to compare agent outputs
A method to temporarily disable tools
A type of AI agent update

exercises.explain: A circuit breaker is a method that protects AI agents by temporarily disabling their tools when they are overloaded or malfunctioning.

cards.title: What is Shadow Testing?
cards.body: Shadow testing is when we run a new version of an AI agent alongside the current version. It helps us see how both versions perform with real user data.

cards.title: Shadow Testing Definition
cards.body: Shadow testing involves parallel operation of a new agent version with the production version. This enables comparison of their outputs without affecting users.

cards.title: Analogy: Split Testing
cards.body: Think of shadow testing like taste testing a new recipe while still serving the original dish. You see how the new version performs without impacting the guests' meal.

cards.title: Example of Shadow Testing
cards.body: When a new AI agent version is released, it runs next to the old version. By comparing their results, we can check if the new version works better.

cards.title: Myth: Shadow Testing is Risky
cards.body: Many believe shadow testing can confuse users, but it is designed to observe without changing their experience.

cards.title: Visualize Shadow Testing
cards.body: See how two different agent versions operate side by side with real traffic.

exercises.prompt: What is one benefit of shadow testing?

options.text: It hides the new version from users
It allows performance comparison with real traffic
It eliminates the need for updates
It only tests in offline environments

exercises.explain: Shadow testing's primary benefit is it allows us to compare the performance of the new version with the old one using real, live user traffic.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Shadow testing can impact the user experience immediately.
exercises.explain: False, shadow testing is designed to observe performance without changing the user experience.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Shadow testing runs a new agent version in __________ with the production version.
exercises.sentence: Shadow testing runs a new agent version in parallel with the production version.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'parallel', meaning that both versions operate side by side at the same time.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes shadow testing?

options.text: Running different agents separately in different environments
Testing a new agent version live alongside its previously deployed version
Only testing the new version without real user data

exercises.explain: Shadow testing requires the new agent version to operate live alongside the existing version to capture real-time performance data.

exercises.prompt: Why is shadow testing important for AI agents?

options.text: It replaces the old version instantly
It helps verify improvements before full deployment
It requires no monitoring after updates
It is only used when failures occur

exercises.explain: Shadow testing is critical because it allows us to verify that the new version is better before fully replacing the old version, ensuring a smooth transition and maintaining user satisfaction.
